title: aspidistra cyathiflora subsp. bifolia
intro: Aspidistra cyathiflora subsp. bifolia, also known as the 'Cast Iron Plant', is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Asparagaceae. Traditionally, the Cast Iron Plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as acting as an antiseptic and anti-parasitic agent. In herbal preparations, the Cast Iron Plant is often used in tea form, and its rhizomes can be dried and crushed for use in infusions, tinctures, or poultices.
